field_label:
Welcome to the Cycle Study!

We’d like to start by asking some questions about you.

Then, we will ask you to complete daily and weekly Questionnaires for two menstrual cycles, or 10 weeks, whichever comes first.

If you have any questions, please contact us at cyclestudy@kcl.ac.uk.
These questions are about you. There are no right or wrong answers. You can put "Prefer not to answer”. Please click 'Next' to begin the questionnaire
What is your gender identity?
If you selected 'Other' for your gender identity, please specify:
What is your age?

Ethnicity: (These are the government recommended categories for measuring ethnicity. However, we appreciate that they may  not accurately represent everyone. Please feel free to use a text-box to self-define.)

field_label: The next questions are about your experience of PMDD. There are no right or wrong answers. This will help us understand how your experience of PMDD relates to questions logged in your daily and weekly questionnaires and your wearable data. Please answer each question as best as you can. There will be opportunities to share your identity or experiences using free-text boxes.
When did you first receive a diagnosis of PMDD? (Please insert the approximate date, month, and / or year)

field_label: Are you currently using hormonal contraception?
field_note: Logic to be added - if yes, ineligible for participation

field_label: Are you currently on antidepressant medication?
Do you experience some or any of the following premenstrual symptoms which start before your period and stop within a few days of bleeding?
Depressed mood or hopelessness
Anxiety or tension
Tearfulness or increased sensitivity to rejection
Anger or irritability
Decreased interest in work activities
Decreased interest in home activities
Decreased interest in social activities
Difficulty concentrating
Fatigue or lack of energy
Overeating or food cravings
Insomnia
Hypersomnia
Feeling overwhelmed or out of control
Physical symptoms (e.g. breast tenderness, bloating)
Have your symptoms interfered with your work efficiency or productivity?
Have your symptoms interfered with your relationships with coworkers?
Have your symptoms interfered with your relationships with your family?
Have your symptoms interfered with your social life activities?
Have your symptoms interfered with your home responsibilities?

The following questions ask about thoughts, feelings and behaviours, often tied to concerns about family, health, finances, and work. Please answer each question as best as you can. How often have you been bothered by the following over the past 2 weeks? (Click Next to begin)
Little interest or pleasure in doing things?
Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless?
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much?
Feeling tired or having little energy?
Poor appetite or overeating?
Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down?
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television?
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or so fidgety or restless that you have been moving a lot more than usual?

Thoughts that you would be better off dead, or thoughts of hurting yourself in some way? For support, you can reach out to the Samaritans by calling 116 123 or emailing jo@samaritans.org for a reply within 24 hours. Click here for more information.
field_note: NB INSERT SKIP LOGIC IF SEVERAL DAYS TO NEARLY EVERY DAY

field_label:
If you need someone to talk to, you can call the Samaritans on 116 123 or Nightline on 0207 631 0101.

In an emergency, please dial 999 or 112.

Click here for a link to create a suicide safety plan.
Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Trouble relaxing
Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
Becoming easily annoyed or irritable
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen
If you checked any items, how difficult have they made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?

Thank you for taking the time to complete this initial questionnaire.

If you need someone to talk to, you can call the Samaritans on 116 123 or Nightline on 0207 631 0101.

Please reach out to the Cycle Study team if you have any questions: cyclestudy@kcl.ac.uk.

We now invite you to begin logging your Daily and Weekly Questionnaires by clicking 'Finish' and returning to the home page.
